claims.claim: EOUMetrics.end_of_utterance_delay = time between end of speech from VAD and decision to end user's turn
claims.found: class EOUMetrics... end_of_utterance_delay: float / "Amount of time between the end of speech from VAD and the decision to end the user's turn." (lines 94, 97-99)

claims.claim: Vapi: latency-to-response = duration between user's end of statement and agent's start of statement
claims.found: vapi-how-we-solved-latency.md:5 'latency-to-response = duration between user's **end of statement** and agent's **start of statement**. This cycle = **turn-taking**.'

claims.claim: Modal: v2v = duration from user stops speaking to first hearing the bot
claims.found: modal-one-second-voice-to-voice.md:26 'Figure of merit = duration from user stops speaking to first hearing the bot.'

claims.claim: Modal: natural conversation v2v can be ~100ms; apps target ~1 second or less
claims.found: modal...:26 '**Natural conversation v2v latency can be as short as 100ms.** Apps target ~**1 second or less**.'

claims.claim: Modal records full conversation and uses Pyannote diarization to find turn boundaries, aggregating v2v across eCDF
claims.found: modal...:46 'Use **Pyannote (Precision-2)** for speaker diarization to find start/end of each speaker's turns -> compute/aggregate v2v latency.' and :47 'v2v latency eCDFs across deployments.'

claims.claim: Together frames v2v as the hard real-time constraint that forces every downstream model-size and engine choice
claims.found: together...md:5-6 'Real-time: humans respond in ~300ms... 1-2s -> people hang up'; :23 'This budget dictates **model size sweet spot: 8-30B params.**'; :47 'Total v2v budget is the constraint.' Fair paraphrase.

summary: All 6 checkable assertions verified against ground truth. Code_ref to LiveKit EOUMetrics at base.py:94-99 is exact. All three source-attributed figures (Vapi end-of-statement/start-of-statement definition, Modal's 100ms/~1s figures + Pyannote eCDF method, Together's real-time-constraint framing) match the captures verbatim or as fair paraphrase. No WRONG or MISCITED claims.
